---
title: "Input and Network QC for Ancient Metagenomic WGCNA"
subtitle: "A grounded walkthrough using current repository outputs"
author: "Project practical"
date: "2026-05-19"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    css: styles.css
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    margin: 0.06
    slide-number: c/t
    transition: none
    controls: true
    progress: true
    self-contained: false
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Scientific Question

Can we build a stable, biologically interpretable microbial network from damaged ancient DNA?

Key practical challenge:

::: {.callout}
The strongest input PC is associated with detected taxa and read depth. We need to separate technical/preservation structure from biological structure before trusting downstream WGCNA modules and HMM states.
:::

## Repository Map

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A[data + scripts/01_data_prep.R] --> B[scripts/02_wgcna.R]
  B --> C[scripts/02b_wgcna_stability.R]
  C --> D[scripts/03_hmm_states.R]
  A --> E[InputQC/]
  B --> F[networkQC/]
  E --> G[Input transforms, rarefaction, ST8 low-taxa review]
  F --> H[Parameter sweep, full eval, kME, topology]
  H --> I[Recommended WGCNA setting: exp3]
```

::: {.caption}
The main pipeline remains separate from contained QC analyses. InputQC and networkQC are decision-support folders.
:::

## What Each QC Area Answers

| Area | Main question | Current outcome |
|---|---|---|
| `InputQC/` | Is input structure dominated by depth/taxa detection? | PC1 strongly tracks detected taxa/read depth |
| `networkQC/` | Which WGCNA parameters produce stable modules? | `exp3` is the best balanced setting |
| `scripts/02b_wgcna_stability.R` | Do modules recur under resampling? | Bootstrap Jaccard and preservation summarize confidence |
| `scripts/03_hmm_states.R` | How many ecological states are supported? | Hybrid K selection favors stable held-out states |

## Run Order: Practical View

1. `scripts/01_data_prep.R`: build count-derived inputs and metadata.
2. `scripts/02_wgcna.R`: consensus modules across ST8, ST13, GeoB R1; hold out GeoB R2.
3. `scripts/02b_wgcna_stability.R`: bootstrap module assignment stability.
4. `networkQC/`: sweep and evaluate WGCNA hyperparameters.
5. `InputQC/`: stress-test input artifacts and low-detection samples.
6. `scripts/03_hmm_states.R`: HMM state discovery from module eigengenes.

## Main InputQC Finding

From `InputQC/INPUT_QC_DIAGNOSTIC_REPORT.md`:

| Variant / PC | Covariate | Pearson r | Spearman rho |
|---|---|---:|---:|
| current PC1 | detected taxa | -0.987 | -0.980 |
| current PC1 | log total reads | -0.894 | -0.906 |
| current PC1 | Shannon raw | -0.776 | -0.774 |
| sample CLR PC1 | detected taxa | 0.844 | 0.815 |

::: {.warn}
Changing the transform alone did not remove the detection/depth axis. CLR normalizes observed composition; it cannot recover taxa that were never observed.
:::

## Input Ordination: Depth Axis

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="58%"}
![](../InputQC/results/figures/ordination_current_by_depth.png)
:::
::: {.column width="42%"}
What to notice:

- Low-read samples cluster together.
- PC1 is almost a detected-taxa axis.
- This is diagnostic, not an exclusion rule.
- Filtering by PC position would risk circular reasoning.
:::
:::

## Low-Detection Structure

From `InputQC/LOW_DETECTION_STRUCTURE_REPORT.md`:

| Group | n |
|---|---:|
| ST8 | 27 |
| ST13 | 4 |
| GeoB25202_R1 | 4 |
| GeoB25202_R2 | 2 |

Primary rule: `total_reads < 100000 OR detected_taxa < 250`.

::: {.callout}
The low-detection group spans 4.75-133.78 kyr. It is ST8-enriched but not ST8-exclusive, so it may mix technical weakness, preservation, and real paleoenvironmental signal.
:::

## Reads, Taxa, and Shannon Through Time

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../InputQC/results/figures/reads_by_age_core.png)
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../InputQC/results/figures/shannon_by_age_core.png)
:::
:::

::: {.caption}
The same ST8 intervals that have low reads also tend to have low diversity.
:::

## Rarefaction / Depth QC

From `InputQC/rarefaction_depth_qc/RAREFACTION_DEPTH_QC_REPORT.md`:

| QC class | n | pct | median reads | median observed taxa |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| adequate depth | 177 | 82.7 | 511586 | 541 |
| low diversity but saturated | 34 | 15.9 | 41495 | 108.5 |
| insufficient for call | 3 | 1.4 | 6403 | 20 |

::: {.ok}
Most low-read / low-taxa samples do not look like classic still-rising rarefaction failures in the processed matrix.
:::

## Rarefaction Caveat

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="56%"}
![](../InputQC/rarefaction_depth_qc/results/figures/depth_richness_qc_classes.png)
:::
::: {.column width="44%"}
Important warning:

- `phyloseq`/`vegan` reported no singletons.
- Chao1 is weak when singleton information is absent.
- We therefore interpret rarefaction slope and observed richness more than Chao1.
:::
:::

## Core / Age Imbalance

From `InputQC/core_age_imbalance/CORE_AGE_IMBALANCE_REPORT.md`:

| Core | n | age span kyr | samples / 10 kyr | low-detection % |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| GeoB25202_R1 | 26 | 146.51 | 1.77 | 15.4 |
| GeoB25202_R2 | 26 | 146.51 | 1.77 | 7.7 |
| ST13 | 48 | 142.43 | 3.37 | 8.3 |
| ST8 | 118 | 106.16 | 11.12 | 22.9 |

ST8 is much more densely sampled, so age-window fairness matters.

## Age-Bin Imbalance: 90-110 kya

| Age band | Core | n | median reads | median taxa | low-detection % |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 90-110 kya | GeoB R1 | 4 | 580310 | 593 | 0.0 |
| 90-110 kya | GeoB R2 | 4 | 730409 | 600 | 0.0 |
| 90-110 kya | ST13 | 4 | 832988 | 710 | 0.0 |
| 90-110 kya | ST8 | 16 | 42592 | 110 | 93.8 |

::: {.danger}
The strongest concern is not generic ST8 sampling density; it is a specific ST8 interval where low detection is enriched beyond the other cores.
:::

## Imbalance Figures

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../InputQC/core_age_imbalance/results/figures/core_age_coverage_heatmap.png)
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../InputQC/core_age_imbalance/results/figures/low_detection_fraction_heatmap.png)
:::
:::

## ST8 Low-Taxa Review

Definition: ST8 samples with `detected_taxa < 250`.

| Group | n | median library conc. | median reads | median taxa | median Shannon |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| ST8 low taxa | 27 | 36.23 | 39371 | 109 | 4.00 |
| ST8 not low taxa | 91 | 99.94 | 361920 | 508 | 5.39 |
| non-ST8 | 100 | 93.77 | 619230 | 558 | 5.05 |

::: {.callout}
Library concentration contributes strongly, but it does not explain every low-taxa ST8 sample.
:::

## Are Reads Collapsed Into Few Taxa?

From `InputQC/st8_low_taxa_review/ST8_LOW_TAXA_REVIEW.md`:

| Metric | Median |
|---|---:|
| top taxon read share | 11.7% |
| top 5 taxa read share | 36.0% |
| top 10 taxa read share | 49.7% |
| taxa needed for 80% reads | 45 |
| Pielou evenness | 0.87 |

Most samples are not single-taxon collapses, but the sparsest ~100-103 kya samples are concentrated.

## ST8 Low-Taxa Figures

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../InputQC/st8_low_taxa_review/results/figures/st8_low_taxa_read_concentration_by_age.png)
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../InputQC/st8_low_taxa_review/results/figures/st8_low_taxa_top_taxa_heatmap.png)
:::
:::

## WGCNA: Main Construction Logic

`scripts/02_wgcna.R`

```r
# lines 4-6
# Consensus across ST8, ST13, GeoB25202_R1.
# GeoB25202_R2 held out for preservation testing.
# Soft power selected by signed R² (negative slope required).
```

```r
# lines 119-130
blockwiseConsensusModules(
  multiExpr = multiExpr, power = soft_power,
  networkType = "signed", corType = "pearson",
  minModuleSize = PARAMS$wgcna_min_module_size,
  deepSplit = PARAMS$wgcna_deep_split,
  mergeCutHeight = PARAMS$wgcna_merge_cut_height
)
```

## WGCNA Concepts Students Need

| Object | Plain meaning |
|---|---|
| correlation matrix | taxa that rise/fall together |
| adjacency | correlation transformed by soft power |
| TOM | shared-neighbor similarity; more stable than raw edges |
| dynamic tree cut | finds modules from TOM dissimilarity |
| eigengene | first PC summarizing each module |
| grey | unassigned/technical catch-all, not automatically biological |

## Preservation and Age-Aligned Concordance

`scripts/02_wgcna.R`

```r
# lines 183-193: R1 -> R2 preservation
modulePreservation(
  multiData = list(GeoB_R1 = ..., GeoB_R2 = ...),
  multiColor = list(GeoB_R1 = module_colors[good]),
  nPermutations = preservation_perms,
  randomSeed = PARAMS$seed
)
```

```r
# lines 257-264: common age grid
xout <- seq(age_min, age_max, length.out = PARAMS$wgcna_stability_age_grid_points)
y1 <- approx(d1$age_kyr, d1$value, xout = xout)$y
y2 <- approx(d2$age_kyr, d2$value, xout = xout)$y
```

## Bootstrap Stability

`scripts/02b_wgcna_stability.R`

```r
# lines 82-88: resample samples within each training core
idx <- sample(seq_len(nrow(dat)), size = nrow(dat), replace = TRUE)
dat[idx, , drop = FALSE]
```

```r
# lines 143-154: match each original module to best bootstrap module
jaccard = intersection(original, bootstrap) / union(original, bootstrap)
```

::: {.callout}
Bootstrap stability asks whether module membership survives sample perturbation, not whether a module is biologically important.
:::

## Original WGCNA Stability Output

From `results/wgcna_stability/WGCNA_STABILITY_REPORT.md`:

| module | median Jaccard | p05 | p95 | baseline size |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| brown | 0.554 | 0.269 | 0.819 | 85 |
| turquoise | 0.475 | 0.281 | 0.729 | 237 |
| blue | 0.373 | 0.000 | 0.617 | 138 |
| yellow | 0.314 | 0.149 | 0.515 | 76 |
| green | 0.039 | 0.000 | 0.566 | 65 |

This motivated the deeper `networkQC/` parameter sweep.

## networkQC: Parameter Sweep

`networkQC/scripts/02_wgcna_parameter_sweep.R`

```r
# lines 30-35
grid <- CJ(
  power = c(12, 16, 20),
  deepSplit = c(1, 2, 3),
  mergeCutHeight = c(0.10, 0.15, 0.20, 0.25),
  minModuleSize = c(20, 30)
)
```

Sweep scoring first emphasized:

- lower grey burden,
- enough non-grey modules,
- reasonable module size.

Full evaluation then added preservation, bootstrap, concordance, and core balance.

## Full Candidate Evaluation

From `networkQC/results/full_eval/FULL_EVAL_REPORT.md`:

| rank | setting | params | grey % | boot Jaccard | strong preserved | Pearson | balanced Jaccard |
|---:|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 1 | exp3 | p12 d3 merge0.25 min20 | 28.66 | 0.400 | 6 | 0.857 | 0.517 |
| 2 | exp4 | p12 d3 merge0.20 min20 | 28.66 | 0.378 | 7 | 0.859 | 0.459 |
| 3 | opt5 | p12 d1 merge0.20 min20 | 34.06 | 0.395 | 5 | 0.714 | 0.456 |
| 10 | baseline | p20 d2 merge0.15 min20 | 66.56 | 0.351 | 4 | 0.847 | 0.349 |

## Full Eval Heatmaps

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../networkQC/results/figures/full_eval_setting_metric_heatmap.png)
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../networkQC/results/figures/full_eval_module_metric_heatmap.png)
:::
:::

## kME and Topology Checks

`networkQC/scripts/10_kme_topology_review.R`

```r
# lines 85-87
mes <- orderMEs(moduleEigengenes(expr, colors = colors)$eigengenes)
kme <- cor(expr, mes, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")
```

```r
# lines 156-160
adj <- adjacency(train_expr, power = power, type = "signed")
tom <- TOMsimilarity(adj, TOMType = "signed")
diag(tom) <- 0
```

kME checks membership quality. TOM checks whether modules align with network topology.

## Integrated QC Ranking

From `networkQC/results/KME_TOPOLOGY_QC_REPORT.md`:

| rank | setting | full eval | kME | topology | integrated | grey % | median kME | TOM separation | flags |
|---:|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 1 | exp3 | 0.926 | 0.476 | 0.418 | 0.753 | 28.66 | 0.703 | 3.532 | 0 |
| 2 | exp4 | 0.868 | 0.309 | 0.034 | 0.588 | 28.66 | 0.710 | 3.037 | 0 |
| 3 | opt5 | 0.546 | 0.408 | 0.000 | 0.353 | 34.06 | 0.689 | 2.487 | 0 |
| 4 | baseline | 0.227 | 0.714 | 0.703 | 0.319 | 66.56 | 0.783 | 48.011 | 1 |

::: {.ok}
Decision: keep `exp3` as the best current setting; use `opt5` as conservative fallback.
:::

## Graph Layout Diagnostics

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../networkQC/results/figures/layout_diagnostics/opt5_nongrey_nonisolates_fr.png)
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../networkQC/results/figures/kme_topology_setting_heatmap.png)
:::
:::

::: {.caption}
Graph plots are visualization filters on TOM edges; they are not how WGCNA modules are constructed.
:::

## HMM State Discovery

`scripts/03_hmm_states.R`

```r
# lines 4-10
1. residualise eigengenes within core
2. PCA on training cores; project validation/all samples
3. fit K = 2..5 HMMs
4. score held-out validation core
5. select K using BIC + held-out likelihood/stability
```

Hybrid logic:

- train BIC narrows plausible K,
- held-out log likelihood picks predictive K,
- transition stability penalizes noisy state switching.

## Current ST8 Removal Sensitivity

`InputQC/scripts/03_st8_low_taxa_network_sensitivity.R`

Current status at deck creation:

| setting | status |
|---|---|
| exp3 | complete |
| exp4 | preservation complete; bootstrap running |
| opt5 | not yet started |

::: {.warn}
The run is intentionally contained in `InputQC/` and does not alter the main pipeline. It tests whether removing 27 ST8 low-taxa samples changes the exp3/exp4/opt5 decision.
:::

## What We Trust Now

1. The input has a real detection/depth axis.
2. The low-detection samples are not random noise.
3. Rarefaction does not show broad undersequencing in the processed matrix.
4. ST8 has a specific 90-110 kya low-detection issue.
5. WGCNA parameter choice improved substantially over baseline.
6. `exp3` is the current best network setting.

## How to Recognize Bad Analysis Signatures

::: {.danger}
Warning signs:
:::

- PC1 almost perfectly tracks read depth or detected taxa.
- Grey module is huge and biologically tempting.
- Module stability depends on a single core or age interval.
- Rarefaction/Chao1 are interpreted after singleton-removing filters without caution.
- Network plots are treated as module construction evidence.

## Practical Output Checklist

| Folder | Key outputs |
|---|---|
| `InputQC/results/figures/` | PC-depth heatmaps and ordinations |
| `InputQC/rarefaction_depth_qc/` | rarefaction classes and depth/richness figures |
| `InputQC/core_age_imbalance/` | age/core imbalance and sample winnowing |
| `InputQC/st8_low_taxa_review/` | low-taxa ST8 read/taxon diagnostics |
| `networkQC/results/full_eval/` | ranked WGCNA settings |
| `networkQC/results/tables/` | kME, topology, final integrated ranking |

## Final Recommendation

::: {.ok}
Use `exp3`: `power=12`, `deepSplit=3`, `mergeCutHeight=0.25`, `minModuleSize=20`.
:::

Rationale:

- lowest grey burden among top candidates,
- best integrated full-eval / kME / topology score,
- best core-balance Jaccard among top candidates,
- no kME/topology warning flags.

Continue treating input depth as a standing caveat until the ST8 removal sensitivity finishes.
